xi_cao.name: Xi Cao
xi_cao.summary:
Xi Cao broke through with a tournament title — a moment that separates contenders from champions. Across 49 entries in 21 tournaments, Xi has shown the kind of competitive fire that produces breakthroughs, and there are surely more titles ahead.

A true triple-threat, Xi competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Xi has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 20 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Jonathan Wu — 3 events in BD, best: #3

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 1 title across 4 tournaments — an improvement that shows Xi is still ascending. The best may yet be ahead.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Xi has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Xi isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 11 seeded entries across the career, Xi consistently earns the respect of tournament committees — a testament to the results put up on the court.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title to show for it, Xi is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

Xiaoan Fan is steadily building a competitive resume, with 13 entries across 6 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game, and the results will follow the effort.

Xiaoan is a versatile competitor who takes the court in singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. That willingness to compete across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that many specialists simply cannot match.

Xiaoan has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as the competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience under the belt, Xiaoan brings the kind of tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court and competing against the best.

xiner_hu.name: Xiner Hu
xiner_hu.summary:
Xiner Hu is a U.S. Junior National Champion — a distinction that places Xiner among the finest junior players in the country. With 8 career tournament victories across 10 tournaments and 30 total entries, this is a player who consistently rises to the biggest moments.

A true triple-threat, Xiner competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 3 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Signature achievements: National Champion in GD U11 • 7 ORC titles (across 5 tournaments).

Xiner has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 8 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Naksh Shetty — 8 events together in XD, producing an outstanding 3 titles
  • Sophia Z Liu — 3 events together in GD, producing an outstanding 2 titles

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 6 podium finishes across 4 tournaments. Xiner is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Xiner has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as the competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Xiner has competed across 5 different regions of the country (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 5 seeded entries across the career, Xiner consistently earns the respect of tournament committees — a testament to the results put up on the court.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Xiner's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

xintong_jiang.name: Xintong Jiang
xintong_jiang.summary:
Xintong Jiang is a rising force in junior badminton, already collecting 3 tournament titles among 35 career entries across 15 tournaments. The trajectory is unmistakable — this is a player whose best chapters are still being written.

A true triple-threat, Xintong competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, 1 doubles title, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Xintong has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 13 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Ethan Yip Ly — 3 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Enming Cao — 3 events in XD, best: #4

Having moved up to U15, Xintong is navigating the challenge of tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment period is natural — and with the pedigree Xintong brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Xintong has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Xintong has competed across 4 different regions of the country (NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 3 titles to show for it, Xintong is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

xinyan_zeng.name: Xinyan Zeng
xinyan_zeng.summary:
Xinyan Zeng is a U.S. Junior National Champion — a distinction that places Xinyan among the finest junior players in the country. With 6 career tournament victories across 25 tournaments and 70 total entries, this is a player who consistently rises to the biggest moments.

A true triple-threat, Xinyan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 5 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Signature achievements: National Champion in GD U13, GD U17 • National Finalist in XD U13 • 3 ORC titles (across 3 tournaments) • U.S. Selection Event top-1 finisher.

Xinyan has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 21 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Sophia Jia — 7 events together in GD/U17, producing an outstanding 3 titles
  • Sydney Li — 4 events together in GD/U15, producing an outstanding 3 titles
  • Kevin Zhang — a trusted U15/XD partner (4 events, best result: #2)

A veteran of the junior circuit, Xinyan has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Xinyan has competed across 6 different regions of the country (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 12 seeded entries across the career, Xinyan consistently earns the respect of tournament committees — a testament to the results put up on the court.

With 6 seasons of competitive experience and 6 titles to show for it, Xinyan is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.
